title: Ranunculus sotkamoensis
taxonomy: Ranunculus sotkamoensis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is further categorized under the genus Ranunculus and the species R. sotkamoensis. It belongs to the order Ranunculales, which is a group of flowering plants. The class of this plant is Magnoliopsida, and its phylum is Magnoliophyta. At the most basic level, it is classified as a eudicot.

intro: Ranunculus sotkamoensis is a rare and endemic plant species found in Finland, belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in soothing various skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus sotkamoensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
